worth_it_explanation: Creative scanning is the line between a clean page and one that auto-redirects a reader to a scam, drops malware, or runs a creative the publisher would never have approved by hand. Equativ can scan creatives as they flow through the platform, but scanning only protects the page if it is switched on and if the flagged results are acted on rather than left to keep serving. This check is a review of settings the publisher already controls, so it costs reading time rather than rebuild time, and the finding points at a concrete safety gap a publisher can close before a bad creative reaches an audience.
intro: Ad quality scanning is the publisher's defense against creatives that carry malware, force auto-redirects, or break the policies a publisher sets for its inventory. Equativ, as a sell-side platform and ad server, sits between the demand it connects to and the page a reader loads, which is the point where a creative can be inspected before it serves. This subcategory is about whether that inspection is running and whether the publisher follows through on what it surfaces. These checks confirm that creative scanning is enabled across the inventory it should cover, and that flagged creatives are blocked or escalated rather than left active.

audit_checks.title: Creative scanning for malware and auto-redirects is not enabled
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A creative that auto-redirects a reader to a scam page or attempts a drive-by download reaches the audience the moment it serves, and the publisher wears the reputational damage and the user-trust loss. When scanning is off, nothing inspects the creative before it renders, so a malicious or policy-violating ad serves freely. The harm lands directly on the reader and the publisher's brand, which is why this sits higher than a routine configuration gap.

educate.base:
Creative scanning inspects an ad before, or as, it serves and looks for behavior the publisher does not want on the page. The behaviors that matter most are the ones that act without the reader's consent. An auto-redirect throws the reader off the page to a destination they never chose, often a scam or a fake prize page. A drive-by download tries to push a file onto the reader's device. A creative may also carry code that opens a modal, hijacks the back button, or calls out to a domain the publisher has no relationship with. Scanning is the inspection step that catches these before they reach an audience.

Equativ is a sell-side platform and ad server, so it sits between the demand it connects to and the page a reader loads. That position is where a creative can be inspected because every creative flows through the platform on its way to the page. Equativ provides ad quality controls in its console for this purpose, and the audit question is whether the publisher has those controls switched on rather than serving demand unscanned.

Scanning works alongside the rendering protection a secure ad container provides, rather than replacing it. The Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Tech Lab built SafeFrame to sandbox a creative inside a managed iframe so that behaviors such as auto-redirects and unwanted downloads are restricted at render time. Scanning catches a bad creative earlier, before it serves, while the container limits what a creative can do once it renders. A publisher gets the strongest protection when both are in place.

A few signs point to scanning being off or incomplete:
  • The Equativ ad quality or creative scanning controls are left at a default the publisher cannot confirm is enabled
  • Scanning covers some inventory or some demand connections but not the open-market demand where unknown creatives arrive
  • The publisher has had auto-redirect or malware complaints from readers with no scanning record showing what served
You may want to confirm that creative scanning is enabled across the inventory and demand that can carry unknown creatives. One approach is to review the ad quality settings in the Equativ console, check which demand connections feed scanned versus unscanned paths, and turn scanning on wherever an unknown creative could otherwise serve unchecked.

site_type_overlays.publishing: On a media site, an auto-redirect or a malware creative damages reader trust directly since the audience associates the bad experience with the publication rather than with the advertiser. You may want to confirm scanning covers the open-market demand that fills remnant inventory because that is where an unknown creative is most likely to arrive without a direct relationship behind it.
site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a commerce-content site, a creative that redirects a shopper away mid-session costs a sale as well as trust, and a fake-prize redirect on a checkout-adjacent page is the kind of experience that drives a buyer off for good. You may want to confirm scanning is enabled on the commercial pages specifically, where a hijacked session has a direct revenue cost beyond the reputational one.

learn_more.source_title: IAB Tech Lab Releases SafeFrame 2.0 Secure Ad Container

educate.learn_more_note: Equativ's detailed configuration documentation for ad quality and creative scanning is in the partner portal (login required).

investigate.intro: Review the ad quality settings in the Equativ console to confirm creative scanning is enabled, and map which demand connections feed scanned versus unscanned paths to the page.

equativ_console.label: Equativ console
steps: Open the ad quality or creative controls section of the Equativ console and record whether creative scanning is enabled.
Note which scanning categories are active, for example malware, auto-redirect, and policy violations, and which are left off.
Map each demand connection to whether its creatives pass through scanning before they serve, and flag any open-market or remnant demand that reaches the page unscanned.
Ask the publisher whether any reader complaints of auto-redirects or malware have come in, and record what scanning was in place at the time.

manual_inspection.label: Manual page inspection
steps: Load representative pages across the inventory and watch for any creative that redirects the page, opens a modal, or attempts a download without interaction.
Use the browser developer tools network panel to note any creative that calls out to an unexpected domain or initiates a navigation the reader did not trigger.
Record any suspect creative behavior alongside the page and placement where it appeared so it can be matched to the demand path that served it.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether creative scanning is enabled in the Equativ console, which scanning categories are active, and whether every demand path that can carry an unknown creative passes through scanning before serving. A setup where scanning is on and covers the open-market and remnant demand passes. A setup where scanning is off, or where unknown creatives reach the page on an unscanned path, is a fail recorded with the demand connections and inventory left unprotected.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether creative scanning is enabled and which demand paths it covers above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} serves demand without creative scanning enabled across every path that can carry an unknown creative. Without scanning, nothing inspects a creative before it renders, so an auto-redirect, a drive-by download, or a policy-violating ad can reach a reader and the harm lands on the publisher's brand and the reader's trust. {{voice_recommend}} enabling creative scanning in the Equativ console for malware, auto-redirects, and policy violations, then confirming the open-market and remnant demand that fills unknown creatives passes through scanning before it serves. Pairing scanning with a secure ad container such as SafeFrame adds rendering protection on top of the pre-serve inspection.

audit_checks.title: Flagged creatives are left active rather than blocked or escalated
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Scanning that flags a bad creative but does not stop it from serving gives a false sense of safety since the report exists while the creative keeps reaching readers. A flag with no follow-through means the publisher has the evidence of a problem and still wears the harm, so the gap is in the response rather than the detection.

educate.base:
Detection is only half of creative quality control. The other half is what happens after a creative is flagged. Scanning can surface a creative that auto-redirects or violates policy, but if that flag does not lead to the creative being blocked, the demand path being throttled, or the issue being escalated to the demand partner, the creative keeps serving and the flag becomes a record of harm rather than a stop on it.

Equativ surfaces flagged creatives in its ad quality reporting, which gives the publisher the raw material to act. Acting means one of a few things depending on the platform's controls. A specific creative can be blocked so it stops serving. A demand connection that repeatedly sends bad creatives can be throttled or paused while the problem is worked. A pattern can be escalated to the demand partner so the source is addressed rather than each creative caught one at a time. The audit question is whether a flag triggers any of these or whether flagged creatives sit in a report while they continue to reach the page.

The response also needs an owner and a cadence. A flag that no one reviews until the next quarterly audit is functionally the same as no flag at all for the days or weeks the creative serves in between. A publisher gets value from scanning when someone is responsible for reviewing flags on a regular schedule and has a clear path to block or escalate what the scan surfaces.

A few signs point to flags that are not acted on:
  • The ad quality report shows flagged creatives that are still serving days or weeks after the flag
  • No one is named as responsible for reviewing scanning results, so flags accumulate without a response
  • Repeat offenders among the demand connections keep sending flagged creatives with no throttle, pause, or escalation on record
You may want to confirm that a flagged creative triggers a real response rather than just a report entry. One approach is to assign an owner for reviewing scanning results on a set cadence, with a defined path to block the creative, throttle the demand connection, or escalate to the partner depending on what the flag shows.

site_type_overlays.publishing: On a media site, a flagged creative that keeps serving while it sits in a report reaches readers for as long as the gap between the flag and the response lasts. You may want to set a short review cadence for scanning results because the reputational cost of a bad creative on an editorial page accrues every day it continues to serve.
site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a commerce-content site, a flagged creative left active on a high-intent page keeps costing sessions and sales until someone blocks it. You may want to prioritize the response on commercial and checkout-adjacent inventory, where the delay between a flag and a block has a direct revenue cost on top of the trust cost.

learn_more.source_title: IAB Tech Lab Releases SafeFrame 2.0 Secure Ad Container

educate.learn_more_note: Equativ's detailed configuration documentation for ad quality reporting and creative blocking is in the partner portal (login required).

investigate.intro: Review the ad quality reporting in the Equativ console for flagged creatives that are still serving, and confirm whether an owner and a response path exist for what scanning surfaces.

equativ_console.label: Equativ console
steps: Open the ad quality reporting and list the creatives flagged for malware, auto-redirects, or policy violations over a recent window.
For each flagged creative, check its serving status and note any that are still active after the flag.
Record whether flagged creatives map to repeat demand connections, which would point to a source worth throttling or escalating rather than blocking creative by creative.
Ask the publisher who reviews scanning results, on what cadence, and what action a flag triggers, and record any flag with no response on file.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the flagged creatives over a recent window, their serving status, and whether a defined owner, cadence, and response path exist for acting on them. A setup where flags are reviewed on a set cadence and lead to a block, throttle, or escalation passes. A setup where flagged creatives keep serving with no owner or response path is a fail recorded with the flagged creatives still active and the demand connections behind them.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the flagged creatives, their serving status, and whether a response path exists above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} surfaces flagged creatives in Equativ's ad quality reporting but leaves them active rather than blocking or escalating them. A flag with no follow-through means the evidence of a bad creative exists while the creative keeps reaching readers, so the gap is in the response rather than the detection. {{voice_recommend}} assigning an owner to review scanning results on a set cadence, with a defined path to block the creative, throttle a demand connection that repeats, or escalate the pattern to the demand partner. Acting on flags promptly closes the window between detection and harm that an unactioned report leaves open.
